text:
PET-CT SCAN — WHOLE BODY WITH 18F-FLUORODEOXYGLUCOSE (18F-FDG)

Patient: Marcus R. Ellison
Date of Birth: 1969
Sex: Male
Clinical Indication: Staging of newly diagnosed metastatic melanoma (primary site: upper back). BRAF V600E-mutant. Known hepatic metastases, bilateral axillary and left cervical nodal involvement, and multiple subcutaneous satellite lesions on prior CT. PET-CT obtained for comprehensive metabolic disease characterization and baseline documentation prior to initiation of systemic therapy.

PROCEDURE DETAILS:
The patient was fasted for a minimum of 6 hours prior to radiotracer administration. Blood glucose at time of injection was 94 mg/dL (within acceptable range for FDG PET imaging). Intravenous injection of 18F-FDG was administered with a dose of 12.0 mCi. Uptake period of approximately 60 minutes was observed in a quiet, dimly lit room with the patient resting comfortably in the supine position and kept warm to minimize brown adipose tissue activation. Whole-body PET acquisition was performed from the vertex of the skull to the mid-thighs, combined with low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic correlation. Images were reconstructed using time-of-flight iterative reconstruction with resolution recovery. Fused PET/CT images, PET-only, and CT-only images were reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes.

FINDINGS:

Head and Neck:
The brain demonstrates physiologically symmetric FDG uptake in the cortical and subcortical grey matter structures, without any focal abnormal hypermetabolic activity suspicious for intraparenchymal or leptomeningeal metastatic involvement. No asymmetric FDG-avid lesions are identified in the cerebral hemispheres, cerebellum, or brainstem.

In the neck, there are two discrete hypermetabolic lymph nodes at left cervical level II, measuring approximately 1.8 cm and 2.2 cm on underlying attenuation-correction CT, with maximum standardized uptake values (SUVmax) of 9.2 and 7.6, respectively. A third enlarged node at left level III demonstrates SUVmax of 6.8. The nodal architecture is disrupted and the degree of FDG avidity is markedly above mediastinal blood pool and liver background, in keeping with malignant infiltration. No FDG-avid right cervical or supraclavicular adenopathy is identified. The parotid and submandibular glands show symmetric physiologic uptake. The thyroid gland demonstrates homogeneous background-level uptake without focal uptake.

Chest and Axillae:
Within the right axilla, there is a dominant hypermetabolic conglomerate nodal mass. On co-registered CT images, this measures approximately 4.8 x 3.9 cm with central low-density necrosis. FDG avidity is intense and heterogeneous, with peripheral viable tumor demonstrating SUVmax of 18.7 and the necrotic center showing photopenic areas. This represents by far the most metabolically active lesion identified on this examination. No significant FDG-avid left axillary adenopathy is identified. No hypermetabolic mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes are present. The heart demonstrates normal physiologic myocardial uptake. No FDG-avid pulmonary nodules are identified; the lungs are clear of any metabolically active parenchymal disease.

The primary site on the upper back is notable for a region of intense focal FDG uptake in the midline and paramedian left upper back, corresponding to the known primary melanoma. On underlying CT, the lesion is characterized by a thick, irregular soft tissue density with skin and subcutaneous involvement, measuring approximately 4 cm in greatest dimension. The SUVmax of the primary lesion is 12.4, consistent with a highly metabolically active cutaneous primary tumor. Adjacent skin and immediately surrounding soft tissue demonstrate increased FDG activity extending to the immediate periphery, compatible with the known deep invasive nature and locally advanced character of the primary melanoma (Breslow 6.1 mm, Clark level V).

Abdomen and Pelvis:
The liver is enlarged (craniocaudal span approximately 18 cm). Six distinct foci of abnormal FDG uptake are identified within the hepatic parenchyma, distributed across multiple segments. The dominant hepatic lesion is in segment VII and demonstrates SUVmax of 14.3, with underlying CT showing a 3.1 x 2.7 cm hypodense lesion with peripheral ring enhancement on the co-registered CT attenuation correction images. A second lesion in segment II demonstrates SUVmax of 10.8, measuring 1.8 x 1.5 cm. The four remaining hepatic foci range in size from approximately 0.7 to 1.4 cm and demonstrate SUVmax values between 6.1 and 9.5. The background hepatic parenchyma demonstrates mildly increased FDG uptake relative to expected normal liver parenchyma, potentially reflecting metabolic perturbation from multifocal tumor burden. No FDG-avid biliary ductal involvement. No portal or hepatocaval venous tumor thrombus identified by metabolic or morphologic criteria.

The spleen is normal in size and demonstrates homogeneous physiologic uptake without focal hypermetabolic lesions.

The adrenal glands are unremarkable, with no focal FDG-avid adrenal nodules. The pancreas demonstrates diffuse physiologic uptake. The kidneys demonstrate bilateral symmetric renal physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems without evidence of FDG-avid renal masses.

No FDG-avid intraperitoneal or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy is identified above threshold. The pelvic lymph nodes, iliac chains, and inguinal regions are without abnormal hypermetabolic foci.

Soft Tissue and Musculoskeletal:
At least nine discrete hypermetabolic subcutaneous nodular foci are identified distributed across the trunk and bilateral upper extremities, representing the known satellite and in-transit metastatic disease. The lesions are distributed as follows: multiple foci along the left and right flank and anterior abdominal wall, at least three lesions on the bilateral upper arms, and scattered smaller foci along the posterior trunk. SUVmax values for these subcutaneous nodules range from 4.1 to 8.9. On co-registered CT images, the nodules appear as discrete soft tissue density rounded lesions measuring approximately 0.8 to 1.6 cm. The largest subcutaneous nodule, on the right lateral chest wall, measures 1.4 cm and demonstrates SUVmax of 8.9.

The osseous structures throughout the imaged skeleton, including the axial and appendicular skeleton visualized, demonstrate no focal FDG-avid osseous lesions or sites of abnormal marrow uptake suspicious for skeletal metastatic disease. No lytic or blastic changes are identified on CT osseous windows. Background bone marrow activity is physiologically distributed and symmetric.

No FDG-avid lesions are identified in the peritoneum or omentum. No ascites. No evidence of bowel wall thickening or FDG-avid mesenteric implants.

IMPRESSION:

1. Intensely hypermetabolic primary melanoma of the upper back (SUVmax 12.4), consistent with known high-grade, deeply invasive nodular melanoma.

2. Markedly hypermetabolic right axillary conglomerate nodal mass (SUVmax 18.7, 4.8 x 3.9 cm with central necrosis) representing the dominant site of regional metastatic involvement, with extremely high metabolic activity reflecting aggressive tumor biology.

3. Hypermetabolic left cervical lymphadenopathy (level II-III, SUVmax up to 9.2), in keeping with nodal metastatic disease.

4. Six FDG-avid hepatic metastases distributed throughout multiple hepatic segments, with the dominant lesion in segment VII achieving SUVmax 14.3. The overall hepatic metabolic disease burden is substantial.

5. At least nine discrete hypermetabolic subcutaneous and soft tissue nodules distributed across the trunk and bilateral upper extremities (SUVmax range 4.1-8.9), consistent with the known burden of satellite and in-transit cutaneous/subcutaneous metastatic disease.

6. No FDG-avid intracranial lesions. No evidence of leptomeningeal involvement.

7. No metabolically active osseous lesions. No FDG-avid pulmonary nodules. No mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.

Overall, the findings are consistent with widespread metabolically active systemic metastatic melanoma with involvement of regional and distant lymph nodes, liver (multiple segments), and numerous subcutaneous soft tissue sites. The extent and metabolic activity of disease, particularly the dramatically elevated SUVmax values at multiple nodal and hepatic sites, reflect the high disease burden and aggressive metabolic phenotype of this BRAF-mutant melanoma at presentation. These findings are concordant with the clinical staging of M1c disease without CNS involvement. The PET-CT provides a comprehensive baseline metabolic map for subsequent response assessment following initiation of systemic therapy.

Reviewing Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Nuclear Medicine/PET-CT Division]
Report finalized electronically.
